Headline: Subway trains rerouted to elevated tracks
ShortDescription: Red Line subway trains will be rerouted to the elevated tracks between Cermak-Chinatown and Fullerton in both directions.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
Red Line subway trains will be rerouted to the elevated tracks between Cermak-Chinatown and Fullerton in both directions to allow for subway maintenance.

Trains will not stop at subway stations (see map below).

For fastest travel times, walk to the nearest ‘L’ station served by rerouted trains or use the free bus shuttle service offered between Fullerton and Clark/Lake in both directions.

Allow extra travel time.

*Entry/exit at Merchandise Mart station is via the Kinzie auxiliary entrance stairways, and requires a farecard (no vending machines), during hours when the building is closed. To make step-free transfers to Red Line trains during this time, consider shuttle buses to connect with Clark/Lake or Fullerton.

Headline: #20 Madison Buses Temporary Reroute at Madison/Central Park
ShortDescription: #20 Madison Buses are temporarily rerouted in both directions, due to a street blockage near Madison/Central Park.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
How does this affect my trip?

#20 Madison Buses are temporarily rerouted in both directions via Madison, Central Park, Jackson, Hamlin, and Madison over regular route due to a street blockage near Madison/Central Park.

Allow extra travel time.

Headline: Eastbound #87 87th, #95 95th & #30 South Chicago buses Temporary Reroute near 91st/Burley
ShortDescription: Eastbound #87 87th, #95 95th & #30 South Chicago buses in both directions are temporarily rerouted, due to street a blockage near 91st/Burley.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
How does this affect my trip?

Eastbound #87 87th buses are temporarily rerouted via Buffalo, 92nd, Brandon, 93rd, Baltimore, 91st and over regular route, due to street blockage near 91st/Burley.

Westbound buses are not affected.

Northbound #30 South Chicago buses will operate via Ewing, Harbor Avenue, 93rd, Baltimore, 91st and over regular route.
Southbound #30 South Chicago buses will operate via South Chicago, 93rd, Brandon, and 91st over regular route.

Eastbound #95 95th buses will operate via 92nd, Houston, 93rd, Burley, 91st and over regular route.
Westbound #95 95th will operate via 91st, Brandon, 93rd, Houston, 92nd and over regular route.

Why is service being changed?

Buses are rerouted due to a street blockage near 91st/Burley.

Allow extra travel time.

Headline: Brown and Red Line Service Delayed near Armitage
ShortDescription: Brown and Red Line trains are standing near Armitage due to signal problems. Crews working to restore service.

FullDescription.#cdata-section:
Brown and Red Line trains are standing near Armitage due to signal problems

Consider other service alternatives that may serve your trip, such as nearby bus routes or using buses to other rail lines.

Personnel are working to restore service. Allow extra travel time.
